items.headline: China brokers talks between Pakistan and Afghanistan after border clashes
items.body: Pakistan and Afghanistan's Taliban government resumed peace talks in China's Urumqi after weeks of fighting that killed hundreds. Beijing is mediating between the two sides to broker a lasting ceasefire, with both countries welcoming China's efforts.

items.headline: Cuba pardons over 2,000 prisoners amid pressure from Washington
items.body: Cuba pardoned 2,010 prisoners as a humanitarian gesture during Holy Week, the second such release this year during talks with the Trump administration. Releasing political prisoners has long been a core US demand as Washington enforces an oil blockade on the island.

items.headline: Chinese carriers raise fuel surcharges as oil shock hits aviation
items.body: Air China, China Southern Airlines and subsidiaries announced fuel surcharges will rise by 60 yuan for flights under 800 kilometres and 120 yuan for longer routes starting April 5. Global jet fuel prices more than doubled to $197 per barrel in recent weeks, up from $95.50 a month earlier.

items.headline: Kenya reports 16 nationals missing in Russia after military recruitment
items.body: Kenya's foreign minister reported 16 Kenyans are missing in Russia after joining the country's army, with 38 others in Russian hospitals under restricted access. An estimated 165 Kenyans are actively involved in military operations, bringing the total who have participated in the conflict in Ukraine to over 250.

es.headline: Spain ends fast-track visa program that brought 250,000 Venezuelans since 2018
es.body: The humanitarian scheme allowed Venezuelans one-year renewable permits bypassing standard asylum processes, but authorities said applications overwhelmed the system. Closure shifts Venezuela migrants to same lengthy procedures as other nationalities while the country undergoes political transition after regime change.

no.headline: Norway signs carbon credit agreement with Pakistan under Paris climate framework
no.body: Norway and Pakistan signed their first bilateral carbon market deal enabling emission reduction projects that Norway can purchase as climate credits. The agreement allows Norway to buy international carbon offsets toward climate neutrality goals while channeling finance to Pakistani renewable energy and agriculture sectors.

uk.headline: Britain launches Fair Work Agency with power to investigate employers without worker complaints
uk.body: The Fair Work Agency formally launches, consolidating enforcement of minimum wage, holiday pay and sick pay into a single state authority. The agency can open proactive investigations based on risk assessments rather than waiting for employee complaints.

uk.headline: UK makes sick pay available from day one for all workers regardless of earnings
uk.body: Statutory sick pay becomes payable from the first day of sickness, with the lower earnings limit removed. Employers will face liability earlier and across a broader segment of the workforce.
